channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Menendez family: We suspected abuse from the beginning
item.description: Karen VanderMolen-Copley said the family "knew there was abuse at gut level," but it wasn't something they wanted to believe.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Liam Payne, former One Direction member, dies at 31 in Argentina hotel fall
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Former One Direction singer Liam Payne, 31, whose chart-topping British boy band generated a global following of swooning fans, was found dead Wednesday after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The ballot’s mailed but you die before Election Day. Now what?
item.description: Some states allow them to be counted, and some don't. But most states have never addressed the question.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon, Google make dueling nuclear investments to power data centers with clean energy
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday said that it was investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google, as both tech giants seek new sources of carbon-free electricity to meet surging demand from data centers and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea confirmed Thursday that its recently revised constitution defines South Korea as “a hostile state" for the first time, two days after it blew up front-line road and rail links that once connected the country with the South.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 dead, at least 4 injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and at least four were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, ABC 4

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman accuses Diddy of retaliatory sexual assault in new lawsuit
item.description: A California woman has filed a new lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs, alleging that he and his entourage sexually assaulted her in 2018 as an act of retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: Woman alleges Diddy violently 'gang raped' her in Orinda as form of 'payback'
item.description: A California woman has filed a new lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs, alleging that he and his entourage sexually assaulted her in 2018 as an act of retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Israel professor at Columbia barred from campus after harassing and intimidating other employees
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An outspoken pro-Israel professor at Columbia University has been temporarily barred from campus, a university spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helene and Milton are both likely to be $50 billion disasters, joining ranks of most costly storms
item.description: Monstrous hurricanes Helene and Milton caused so much complex havoc that damages are still being added up, but government and private experts say they will likely join the infamous ranks of Katrina, Sandy and Harvey as super costly $50-billion-plus killers.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-Las Vegas-area Democratic politician gets at least 28 years in prison for killing reporter
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A former Las Vegas-area Democratic elected official was sentenced Wednesday to serve at least 28 years in Nevada state prison for killing an investigative journalist who wrote articles critical of his conduct in office two years ago and exposed an intimate relationship with a female
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of Menendez brothers demands their release from prison
item.description: The family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, as well as their attorney, held a press conference Wednesday to discuss prosecutors’ review of their convictions for murdering their parents, José and Kitty Menendez, in Beverly Hills in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: Robert Telles sentenced for murder of Las Vegas journalist Jeff German
item.description: Robert Telles is accused of plotting to kill Las Vegas Review-Journal reporter Jeff German, 69, after he wrote some unflattering news stories about him that he felt caused him to lose the election.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arrest made in Antioch greenway shooting in less than 24 hours
item.description: Investigative work by Homicide & Specialized Investigations detectives led to the arrest of Paul Park, 29, of Brentwood.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced that it will offer discounted rideshare, bike-share and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot while walking dog in Las Vegas
item.description: Guitarist Jake E. Lee, who formerly played for Ozzy Osbourne, was shot Tuesday while walking his dog, his publicist said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Former Vegas Democrat politician convicted of killing reporter is sentenced to at least 28 years
item.description: Robert Telles, a former Las Vegas-area Democratic politician convicted of killing a journalist, faces at least 28 years in prison before he becomes eligible for parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will headline a rally in Atlanta a week before the Nov. 5 election alongside celebrities and civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until Congress approves new funds
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson assured there would be support to provide necessary funds – when Congress returns after the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Family of Erik and Lyle Menendez call for their release and say they're victims who were vilified
item.description: More than a dozen family members of the convicted Menendez brothers are calling for their release from prison following the 1989 killings of their parents.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 19 mayoral candidates compete to lead Portland, Oregon, in a race with homelessness at its heart
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A lot of political changes are coming to Portland, Oregon. The open mayoral race in the state’s most populous city features 19 candidates — all vying to lead after years of growing frustration over homeless encampments.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NHC monitoring 2 disturbances in the Atlantic and Caribbean | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: With Florida's Gulf Coast left reeling after back-to-back major hurricanes, our Tracking the Tropics team explains what could spare the region from experiencing a third storm within a month.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Man allegedly killed by son,19, in California was renowned photojournalist
item.description: In a bizarre twist to a grisly crime on a popular hiking trail in the San Gabriel Mountains where a 19-year-old man allegedly stabbed his father to death, the victim has been identified as a well-known, award-winning war photographer, photojournalist and professor at the University of the Arts Londo
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Two men shot during Pennsylvania assassination attempt on Trump say Secret Service failed them
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Two men who were shot during the first assassination attempt on Donald Trump this summer say the U.S. Secret Service was “negligent” in protecting the former president and other bystanders at the campaign rally in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new wave of lawsuits
item.description: Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, ABC 4

item.title: Sam's Club opening location in Texas, but there's no checkout lanes
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the warehouse club chain will soon open a store in Texas but there won't be any checkout lanes.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Historic Jersey Shore amusement park closes after generations of family thrills
item.description: OCEAN CITY, N.J. (AP) — For generations of vacationers heading to Ocean City, the towering “Giant Wheel” was the first thing they saw from miles away.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5
